name: Pa'O
notes: Pa'O (Pa-O Karen) of Myanmar, written in the Mon–Burmese (Myanmar) script, an abugida. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified: no consonant/vowel-sign → IPA mapping is claimed here. A correct spec requires the full Myanmar-script akshara model (consonant letters with an inherent vowel, dependent vowel signs, medials, the virama/asat, and the Pa'O-specific tone letters) plus a documented Pa'O phoneme inventory; that research has not been done. Metadata only. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode paok1235), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode paok1235.

ancestors.notes: Sino-Tibetan — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Pa'o Karen.
